title: buxus sempervirens f. rotundifolia
intro: Buxus sempervirens f. rotundifolia, also known as English box or round-leaved boxwood, is a compact, evergreen shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small, rounded leaves and compact growth habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, and its leaves are said to have astringent and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Buxus sempervirens f. rotundifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
